sealed_17b.title: Sealed Corridor 17-B
sealed_17b.summary: Branwen Yards wants a maintenance corridor reopened — sealed eight years ago, never re-cycled. They'll pay for what's still in the toolracks.
sealed_17b.rumour: wire:// somebody pushed past Branwen's old seal on 17-B and didn't come straight back.

narrate: Branwen's mark on the seal is two decades old, half-painted-over. The crack is fresh.
Beyond, the corridor exhales: coolant-sour, dust like old skin, the kind of dark that doesn't read as empty.

choices.label: Push the seal wide and walk in clean
Crack the seal narrow; listen before stepping

narrate: Halfway down 17-B your light catches three cats, or what cats become after eighty unfiltered years.
They are not afraid of you. They are the wrong shape for cats. One of them is breathing with the wrong rhythm.

choices.label: Slip past along the cabling. Ghost.
Back to the seal. Try again next week.

narrate: You stand in the seal-crack and listen.
Three small breaths in 17-B's dark — close together, not synchronised. Something is awake further down.
Knowing first costs less than learning second.

choices.label: Slip in lit-low. You know where they are now.
Burner-page the dock; ask if anyone owes Branwen a favour
Reseal. Tell Branwen the corridor isn't ready.

narrate: The maintenance bay door is at the corridor's far end — keyed lock, a Branwen-blue paint stripe along the seam.
The keypad isn't dead. It's looping, cycling the same three lights in the same order every four seconds.

onSuccess.text: The keypad on 17-B was looping pre-Crisis security — somebody locked it that way on purpose.

choices.label: Leave the door. The corridor itself paid out enough.

narrate: Branwen's tool bay. Iron racks bolted floor to ceiling. Most stripped two decades ago.
One rack isn't. Pre-Crisis hand tools, sealed in a Branwen Yards crate that hasn't been opened.
There's also a Branwen-blue maintenance log on the bench, three pages thick, dated nine days before the Crisis.

choices.label: Take the tools. Leave the log; don't owe the Archive.
Take both. Tamsin will pay for nine days before the Crisis.

effects.text: A Branwen Yards maintenance log dated nine days before the Crisis is now in your hands. Tamsin Coil will want to read this. So will somebody else.
wire:// a sealed Branwen log surfaced and didn't go through the usual Archive channels.

narrate: You reseal the corridor with Branwen's blue. Tools clean, payment routed, no questions.
Hatch behind you holds. Whatever was breathing in there is breathing in there still.
You reseal the corridor. The log is folded against your ribs.
Tamsin will not say thank you. She will know you brought it. The next time you ask her something, the silence before she answers will be a little shorter.
You back out and lock the seal. Branwen will be told the corridor isn't ready.
Nothing taken. Nothing lost.
You back out at the bay door. Whatever's inside stays inside.
Branwen will pay something for the partial scout. Half what they could have.
